question: Create a `Date` class with private members `day`, `month`, and `year` (integers). Implement public setter methods `setDay`, `setMonth`, `setYear` that perform basic validation (e.g., month between 1-12, day between 1-31, ignoring leap years for simplicity). If validation fails, do not update the member and optionally print an error message.

test_cases.input:
Date d1;
std::cout << "d1 (default constructor): " << d1.toString() << std::endl;

test_cases.expected:
Attempting to create d3 with invalid month (15/13/2024):
Error: Invalid month (13). Month must be between 1 and 12. Not updated.
d3: 1/15/2024

Attempting to create d4 with invalid day (32/10/2025):
Error: Invalid day (32). Day must be between 1 and 31. Not updated.
d4: 10/1/2025

Initial d5: 1/1/2000
d5 after valid sets (12/25/2026): 12/25/2026

Attempting to set invalid day (0) on d5:
Error: Invalid day (0). Day must be between 1 and 31. Not updated.
d5 after invalid day set: 12/25/2026

Attempting to set invalid month (100) on d5:
Error: Invalid month (100). Month must be between 1 and 12. Not updated.
d5 after invalid month set: 12/25/2026

Attempting to set valid year (1999) on d5:
d5 after valid year set: 12/25/1999
